description:
Choropleth map displaying data distribution across European countries using a five-step green colour scale, with country borders outlined in red. The geographic area covers EU Member States and neighbouring countries including Iceland, Norway, Switzerland, and Turkey. The legend defines the data classes as follows:
- Light green: 0.24 - 1.09
- Second light green: 1.09 - 1.93
- Medium green: 1.93 - 3.53
- Second dark green: 3.53 - 6.25
- Darkest green: 6.25 - 9.41
Country borders are explicitly marked by red outlines. Countries showing the highest values (6.25 - 9.41, darkest green) include Ireland, Portugal, Belgium, and France. Intermediate to high values (3.53 - 6.25, second dark green) are visible in the United Kingdom, Netherlands, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, Italy, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Lower values (0.24 - 1.09, lightest green) are observed in Iceland, Latvia, Lithuania, Estonia, Albania, North Macedonia, Moldova, parts of Ukraine and Belarus, and Turkey. Countries such as Spain, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Hungary, Romania, Bulgaria, Greece, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Serbia exhibit medium values (1.93 - 3.53). The map indicates a general spatial pattern of higher values in Western Europe and parts of Scandinavia, and comparatively lower values in Eastern and Southeastern Europe.
